proposal: Known-Failures Learning System — SCAR integration, failure suppression, workaround prioritization

findings.gap: store initialized but not operational as learning system

known_failures_store.description: Static catalog from SCAR file — no active failure collection mechanism

agent_ingestion.description: SCAR data not yet ingested into agent configs — agents still attempt config.patch on protected paths

failure_collection.description: No automated mechanism to capture new failures and log them to known-failures.json

recommendations.description: Add scar-openclaw-protected-paths.md references to each agent's workspace-state.md or agent.yaml config. Agents should check SCAR before attempting config.patch on protected paths.
Add GNW boredom scan cycle step: 'Check known-failures.json for unlearned agents. If agents still attempt failed methods, log new entries and update agents_unlearned.'
Each agent should query known-failures.json before config operations. If target path is in suppressed_methods, use priority_workaround instead.

validation_notes: exp_06 validated: infrastructure exists (SCAR file + known-failures.json store) but mechanism not operational as learning system. Gap is between static catalog and active learning. Fix requires SCAR ingestion + failure collection mechanism. This addresses the structural gap identified in cycles 138-142 (10 active proposals with 0 validation evidence).
